message.content:
# KB Page Draft: `react` (npm)

## Package Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package** | `react` |
| **Ecosystem** | npm |
| **Source repository** | [github.com/facebook/react](https://github.com/facebook/react) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Latest stable** | 19.2.5 (as of 2026-04-22) |
| **Maintainers** | `fb <opensource+npm@fb.com>`, `react-bot <react-core@meta.com>` |
| **Weekly downloads** | ~131 million (week of 2026-04-17) |
| **First published** | 2011-10-26 |

## Known Vulnerabilities

### 1. CVE-2013-7035 — XSS via unsanitized keys

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **GHSA** | [GHSA-g53w-52xc-2j85](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-g53w-52xc-2j85) |
| **CVE** | CVE-2013-7035 |
| **CWE** | CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting) |
| **Severity** | MODERATE (CVSS 3.1: AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N) |
| **Affected ranges** | `>=0.4.0 <0.4.2` and `>=0.5.0 <0.5.2` |
| **Fixed versions** | 0.4.2, 0.5.2 |
| **Description** | `react` failed to properly sanitize input used to create keys, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript if a key was generated from user input. |
| **Upstream reference** | [React v0.5.2 / v0.4.2 blog post](https://reactjs.org/blog/2013/12/18/react-v0.5.2-v0.4.2.html) |

### 2. GHSA-hg79-j56m-fxgv — XSS via spoofed React element in `createElement`

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **GHSA** | [GHSA-hg79-j56m-fxgv](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hg79-j56m-fxgv) |
| **CVE** | None listed |
| **CWE** | CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting) |
| **Severity** | HIGH (per GitHub advisory database) |
| **Affected range** | `>=0.0.1 <0.14.0` |
| **Fixed version** | 0.14.0 |
| **Description** | Versions prior to 0.14.0 were vulnerable to XSS because `createElement` did not properly validate its input object, allowing attackers to spoof React elements and execute arbitrary JavaScript. |
| **Upstream reference** | [React v0.14 notable enhancements](https://reactjs.org/blog/2015/10/07/react-v0.14.html#notable-enhancements) |

### Assessment

Both known advisories affect **very old versions** (pre-1.0). The current latest release (19.2.5) and all versions from 0.14.0 onward are **not affected** by either advisory. No additional CVEs or GHSA records were provided for modern react versions.

---

# KB Page Draft: `zod` (npm)

## Package Overview

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Package** | `zod` |
| **Ecosystem** | npm |
| **Source repository** | [github.com/colinhacks/zod](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod) |
| **License** | MIT |
| **Latest stable** | 4.3.6 (as of 2026-01-22) |
| **Maintainers** | `colinhacks <colinmcd94@gmail.com>` |
| **Weekly downloads** | ~161 million (week of 2026-04-17) |
| **First published** | 2020-03-07 |

## Known Vulnerabilities

### 1. CVE-2023-4316 — Denial of Service via email validation ReDoS

| Field | Detail |
|---|---|
| **GHSA** | [GHSA-m95q-7qp3-xv42](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m95q-7qp3-xv42) |
| **CVE** | CVE-2023-4316 |
| **CWE** | CWE-1333 (Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity) |
| **Severity** | MODERATE (CVSS 3.1: AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L) |
| **Affected range** | `>=0 <3.22.3` (last known affected: 3.22.2) |
| **Fixed version** | 3.22.3 |
| **Description** | The email validation regex in Zod was vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS). An attacker could craft a malicious input that causes catastrophic backtracking when validating emails, leading to denial of service. |
| **Fix commit** | [2ba00fe2377f4d53947a84b8cdb314a63bbd6dd4](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/
